title: Can Puck reinvent the news business for the influencer age?
author: Nilay Patel

content.full_text:
Can Puck reinvent the news business for the influencer age? Today, I’m talking with Sarah Personette, CEO of Puck, which is a fancy new media company that’s been around for just about five years now. Puck hires big stars and gives them newsletters that are all mostly part of a subscription bundle. These newsletters ar…
CEO Sarah Personettes big bet on the place where influencers and reporters might meet by Nilay PatelClose Posts from this author will be added to your daily e…

ai_output.summary: Puck is a media company, led by CEO Sarah Personette, that aims to reinvent the news business by blending influencers and reporters. Founded about five years ago, Puck hires notable personalities to create newsletters that are primarily offered through a subscription bundle. The company’s approach focuses on leveraging star power to engage audiences in the evolving media landscape.

title: Did Neuralink make the wrong bet?
author: Elissa Welle

content.full_text:
Did Neuralink make the wrong bet? Elon Musk promised Neuralink would bring superhuman abilities and minds merged with AI. Then he fueled a runaway hype train for his brain implant technology, which ended up with a grisly record for implants in monkeys and some success with human subjects. But…
The field of brain-computer interfaces is moving away from mind-controlled computer cursors to restoring speech. by Elissa WelleClose Posts from this author will be ad…

ai_output.summary: Elon Musk's Neuralink aimed to deliver superhuman abilities and AI-integrated minds but faced challenges, including ethical concerns over monkey implants. While initial hype centered on mind-controlled computer cursors, the brain-computer interface field is now shifting focus toward restoring speech in humans.

title: Anthropic brings Claude into Microsoft Word, and legal contract review leads its use cases
author: Cristian Dina

content.full_text: Anthropic brings Claude into Microsoft Word, and legal contract review leads its use cases In short: Anthropic has released a beta add-in that places Claude directly inside Microsoft Word, with every AI-generated edit appearing as a native tracked change and legal contract review listed first among the tool’s example applications. The add-in, avail… In short: Anthropic has released a beta add-in that places Claude directly inside Microsoft Word, with every AI-generated edit appearing as a native tracked change and legal contract review listed fi…

ai_output.summary: Anthropic has launched a beta add-in that integrates its AI, Claude, directly into Microsoft Word. This add-in enables AI-generated edits to appear as native tracked changes, with legal contract review highlighted as a primary use case.

title: From LLMs to hallucinations, here's a simple guide to common AI terms | TechCrunch
author: Natasha Lomas

content.full_text: From LLMs to hallucinations, here's a simple guide to common AI terms | TechCrunch The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter. Artificial intelligence is a deep and convoluted world. The scientists who work in this field often rely on jargon and lingo to explain what theyre working on. As a result, we frequently have to use …

ai_output.summary: The article provides a glossary of common AI terms and slang that have emerged alongside the rise of artificial intelligence. It aims to clarify jargon used by scientists in the field, helping readers understand key concepts such as LLMs and hallucinations. The guide simplifies the complex language often encountered in AI discussions.

title: Databricks co-founder Matei Zaharia wins ACM Prize and declares AGI is already here
author: Alina Maria Stan

content.full_text: Databricks co-founder Matei Zaharia wins ACM Prize and declares AGI is already here In short: Matei Zaharia, the Berkeley computer science professor and Databricks co-founder who created Apache Spark, has won the 2026 ACM Prize in Computing for his foundational contributions to distributed data systems and AI infrastructure. The $250,000 pri… In short: Matei Zaharia, the Berkeley computer science professor and Databricks co-founder who created Apache Spark, has won the 2026 ACM Prize in Computing for his foundational contributions to dist…

ai_output.summary: Databricks co-founder and Berkeley professor Matei Zaharia has won the 2026 ACM Prize in Computing for his foundational work in distributed data systems and AI infrastructure. Zaharia, creator of Apache Spark, received the $250,000 award and stated that artificial general intelligence (AGI) is already here.

title: WSO2 launches Agent Manager to help enterprises tame AI agent sprawl
author: Duncan Riley

content.full_text: WSO2 launches Agent Manager to help enterprises tame AI agent sprawl Open-source technology provider WS02 LLC today announced the launch of WSO2 Agent Manager, an open control plane for artificial intelligence agents that gives enterprises a unified way to identify, govern, secure and scale agents across environments. The new … Open-source technology provider WS02 LLC today announced the launch of WSO2 Agent Manager, an open control plane for artificial intelligence agents that gives enterprises a unified way to identify, g…

ai_output.summary: WSO2 LLC has launched WSO2 Agent Manager, an open control plane designed to help enterprises manage AI agents. The platform provides a unified way to identify, govern, secure, and scale artificial intelligence agents across different environments.

title: Dyna Software launches Platform Copilot, an agentic AI tool for configuring ServiceNow
author: Duncan Riley

content.full_text: Dyna Software launches Platform Copilot, an agentic AI tool for configuring ServiceNow Dyna Software Inc., a company that provides platform governance and health provider for ServiceNow Inc., today announced the launch of Platform Copilot, an agentic artificial intelligence tool the company describes as the first to autonomously configure, des… Dyna Software Inc., a company that provides platform governance and health provider for ServiceNow Inc., today announced the launch of Platform Copilot, an agentic artificial intelligence tool the c…

ai_output.summary: Dyna Software Inc. has launched Platform Copilot, an agentic AI tool designed to autonomously configure and manage ServiceNow platforms. The company positions this as the first AI tool to provide such autonomous configuration capabilities for ServiceNow.

Inc., today separately announced supply chain solutions aimed at easing business operations. Penske annou…

ai_output.summary: Amazon.com Inc. and Penske Logistics have each announced new supply chain solutions to simplify business operations. Penske introduced Supply Chain Insight, a technology platform designed to enhance logistics management. Both companies aim to improve efficiency in supply chain and transportation services for businesses.

title: The quiet erosion of agency in the age of AI
author: Emre Kazim

content.full_text: The quiet erosion of agency in the age of AI Enterprises are moving fast to embed artificial intelligence into everything from customer interactions to decision-making. The benefits are undeniable: speed, efficiency and scale. The danger isn’t necessarily sudden or dramatic. It’s quieter, more gradual, … Enterprises are moving fast to embed artificial intelligence into everything from customer interactions to decision-making. The benefits are undeniable: speed, efficiency and scale. The danger isnt …

ai_output.summary: Enterprises are rapidly integrating artificial intelligence into various areas such as customer interactions and decision-making, gaining benefits in speed, efficiency, and scale. However, this widespread adoption brings a subtle and gradual risk of eroding human agency rather than sudden or dramatic consequences.

ai_output.summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has acquired Assured Robot Intelligence Inc., a company specializing in artificial intelligence software for robots. The acquisition was announced without disclosure of the financial terms.

author: Maria Deutscher

N… Nebius Group NV, a Dutch operator of artificial intelligence data centers, today announced plans to buy software maker Eigen AI Inc. for $643 million. The company will finance the acquisition with c…

ai_output.summary: Nebius Group NV, a Dutch operator of AI data centers, announced it will acquire AI model optimization startup Eigen AI Inc. for $643 million. The acquisition will be financed through a combination of cash and stock, with the deal expected to close in a few weeks.

They ar… The U.S. Defense Department today announced that it has inked artificial intelligence procurement contracts with seven tech firms. The group includes Amazon Web Service Inc., Google LLC, Microsoft C…

ai_output.summary: The U.S. Defense Department signed AI procurement contracts with seven companies, including Amazon Web Services, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, OpenAI, and SpaceX. Notably, Anthropic was excluded from these deals.

title: Why agentic AI governance is falling short – and what we can do about it
author: Jason Bloomberg

content.full_text: Why agentic AI governance is falling short – and what we can do about it Agentic artificial intelligence misbehavior is reaching epidemic proportions. Today’s AI governance solutions aren’t stopping the madness. We need to rethink our entire approach to AI governance. Even though agentic AI is still nascent, many of the AI agents … Agentic artificial intelligence misbehavior is reaching epidemic proportions. Todays AI governance solutions arent stopping the madness. We need to rethink our entire approach to AI governance. Even…

ai_output.summary: Agentic artificial intelligence misbehavior is becoming widespread, and current AI governance methods are failing to address the problem effectively. The article argues that a complete overhaul of AI governance approaches is necessary to manage and mitigate the risks posed by emerging agentic AI systems.

title: China’s EV price war turns into AI arms race beyond cheaper cars
author: Evelyn Cheng

content.full_text: China’s EV price war turns into AI arms race beyond cheaper cars Chinese EV firms are rapidly rolling out in-car AI features to gain an edge, but as the price war drags on, those technologies are quickly becoming commoditized. BEIJING Electric carmakers in China are layering on more of the same artificial intelligence features as they try to survive a prolonged price war in the world's largest auto market. The competition…

ai_output.summary: Chinese electric vehicle makers are increasingly adding in-car AI features to differentiate themselves amid a prolonged price war in the world's largest auto market. However, as the price competition continues, these AI technologies are rapidly becoming commoditized, diminishing their effectiveness as competitive advantages.

title: Agentic engineering startup JuliaHub lands $65M to automate the design and testing of industrial products
author: Mike Wheatley

content.full_text: Agentic engineering startup JuliaHub lands $65M to automate the design and testing of industrial products JuliaHub Inc., an “agentic” industrial engineering startup that’s trying to automate complex manufacturing processes with artificial intelligence, said today it has raised $65 million in Series B funding. ai_output.summary: JuliaHub Inc., an agentic industrial engineering startup focused on automating complex manufacturing processes using artificial intelligence, has raised $65 million in Series B funding. The funding round was led by Dorilton Capital.

Sequoia and Spark Capital led the round. They were joined by mult…

ai_output.summary: Standard Intelligence Inc., a six-person artificial intelligence startup, has raised $75 million in funding led by Sequoia and Spark Capital. The round also included participation from several angel investors, including notable AI researcher Andrej. The company focuses on developing models for efficient computer use.

title: Featherless.ai pulls in $20M to scale serverless hosting for open-source AI models
author: Duncan Riley

content.full_text: Featherless.ai pulls in $20M to scale serverless hosting for open-source AI models Featherless.ai Inc., a serverless inference platform startup that hosts open-source artificial intelligence models, today revealed it had raised $20 million in new funding to expand its global infrastructure and launch a marketplace for specialized open model… Featherless.ai Inc., a serverless inference platform startup that hosts open-source artificial intelligence models, today revealed it has raised $20 million in new funding to expand its global infras…

ai_output.summary: Featherless.ai, a startup offering a serverless inference platform for hosting open-source AI models, has raised $20 million in new funding. The company plans to use the investment to expand its global infrastructure and launch a marketplace for specialized open models.
